classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

source.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The woman then left her water pot, and went her way into the city, and saith to the men, Come, see a Man which told me all things that ever I did; is not this the Christ?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We require much fervor and uproused zeal, for without these it is impossible to obtain the blessings promised to us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And to show this, Christ at one time saith, “Except a man take up his cross and follow Me, he is not worthy of Me” ( Matt. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 38 ); at another, “I am come to send fire upon the earth, and what will I if it be already kindled?” ( Luke xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 49 ); by both these desiring to represent to us a disciple full of heat and fire, and prepared for every danger.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Such an one was this woman.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For so kindled was she by His words, that she left her water pot and the purpose for which she came, ran into the city, and drew all the people to Jesus.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Come,” she saith, “see a Man which told me all things that ever I did.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe her zeal and wisdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: She came to draw water, and when she had lighted upon the true Well, she after that despised the material one; teaching us even by this trifling instance when we are listening to spiritual matters to overlook the things of this life, and make no account of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For what the Apostles did, that, after her ability, did this woman also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They when they were called, left their nets; she of her own accord, without the command of any, leaves her water pot, and winged by joy performs the office of Evangelists.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And she calls not one or two, as did Andrew and Philip, but having aroused a whole city and people, so brought them to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe too how prudently she speaks; she said not, “Come and see the Christ,” but with the same condescension by which Christ had netted her she draws the men to Him; “Come,” she saith, “see a Man who told me all that ever I did.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: She was not ashamed to say that He “told me all that ever I did.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet she might have spoken otherwise, “Come, see one that prophesieth”; but when the soul is inflamed with holy fire, it looks then to nothing earthly, neither to glory nor to shame, but belongs to one thing alone, the flame which occupieth it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Is not this the Christ?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe again here the great wisdom of the woman; she neither declared the fact plainly, nor was she silent, for she desired not to bring them in by her own assertion, but to make them to share in this opinion by hearing Him; which rendered her words more readily acceptable to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet He had not told all her life to her, only from what had been said she was persuaded (that He was informed) as to the rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nor did she say, “Come, believe,” but, “Come, see”; a gentler expression than the other, and one which more attracted them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou the wisdom of the woman?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: She knew, she knew certainly that having but tasted that Well, they would be affected in the same manner as herself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet any one of the grosser sort would have concealed the reproof which Jesus had given; but she parades her own life, and brings it forward before all men, so as to attract and capture all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “In the mean time His disciples asked Him, saying, Master, eat.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Asked,” here is “besought,” in their native language; for seeing Him wearied with the journey, and the oppressive heat, they entreated Him; for their request concerning food proceeded not from hastiness, but from loving affection for their Teacher?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then saith Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I have meat to eat that ye know not of.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore” (saith the Evangelist) “said the disciples one to another, Hath any man brought Him aught to eat?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why now wonderest thou that the woman when she heard of “water,” still imagined mere water to be meant, when even the disciples are in the same case, and as yet suppose nothing spiritual, but are perplexed? though they still show their accustomed modesty and reverence toward their Master, conversing one with the other, but not daring to put any question to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this they do in other places, desiring to ask Him, but not asking.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then saith Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “My meat is to do the will of Him that sent Me, and to finish His work.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He here calleth the salvation of men “meat,” showing what an earnest desire He hath of providing for us; for as we long for food, so He that we may be saved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And hear how in all places He revealeth not all off-hand, but first throweth the hearer into perplexity, in order that having begun to seek the meaning of what has been said, and then being perplexed and in difficulty, he may when what he sought appears, receive it the more readily, and be made more attentive to listening.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For wherefore said He not at once, “My meat is to do the will of My Father?” (though not even this would have been clear, yet clearer than the other.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I have meat to eat that ye know not of”; for He desireth, as I said, first to make them more attentive through their uncertainty, and by dark sayings like these to accustom them to listen to His words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what is “the will of the Father”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He next speaketh of this, and explaineth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Say ye not, that there are yet four months, and then cometh harvest?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Behold, I say unto you, Lift up your eyes, and look upon the fields, for they are white already to harvest.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Behold, He again by familiar words leadeth them up to the consideration of greater matters; for when He spoke of “meat,” He signified nothing else than the salvation of the men who should come to Him; and again, the “field” and the “harvest” signify the very same thing, the multitude of souls prepared for the reception of the preaching; and the “eyes” of which He speaketh are those both of the mind and of the body; (for they now beheld the crowd of Samaritans advancing;) and the readiness of their will He calleth, “fields already white.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as the ears of corn, when they have become white, and are ready for reaping, so these, He saith, are prepared and fitted for salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And wherefore instead of calling them “fields” and “harvest,” did He not plainly say, that “the men were coming to believe and were ready to receive the Word, having been instructed by the Prophets; and now bringing forth fruit”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What mean these figures used by Him? for this He doth not here only, but through all the Gospel; and the Prophets also employ the same method, saying many things in a metaphorical manner.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then may be the cause of this? for the grace of the Spirit did not ordain it to be so without a reason, but why and wherefore?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On two accounts; one, that the discourse may be more vivid, and bring what is said more clearly before our eyes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the mind when it has laid hold on a familiar image of the matters in hand, is more aroused, and beholding them as it were in a picture, is occupied by them to a greater degree.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is one reason; the other is, that the statement may be sweetened, and that the memory of what is said may be more lasting.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For assertion does not subdue and bring in an ordinary hearer so much as narration by objects, and the representation of experience.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Which one may here see most wisely effected by the parable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And he that reapeth receiveth wages, and gathereth fruit unto life eternal.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the fruit of an earthly harvest profiteth not to life eternal, but to this which is for a time; but the spiritual fruit to that which hath neither age nor death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that the expressions are of sense, but the thoughts spiritual, and that by the very words themselves He divideth things earthly from heavenly?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when in discoursing of water He made this the peculiar property of the heavenly Water, that “he who drinketh it shall never thirst,” so He doth here also when He saith, “that this fruit is gathered unto eternal life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “That both he that soweth and he that reapeth may rejoice together.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Who is “he that soweth”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Who “he that reapeth”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Prophets are they that sowed but they reaped not, but the Apostles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Yet not on this account are they deprived of the pleasure and recompense of their labors, but they rejoice and are glad with us, although they reap not with us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For harvest is not such work as sowing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I therefore have kept you for that in which the toil is less and the pleasure greater, and not for sowing because in that there is much hardship and toil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In harvest the return is large, the labor not so great; nay there is much facility.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By these arguments He here desireth to prove, that “the wish of the Prophets is, that all men should come to Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This also the Law was engaged in effecting; and for this they sowed, that they might produce this fruit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He showeth moreover that He sent them also, and that there was a very intimate connection between the New Covenant and the Old, and all this He effecteth at once by this parable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He maketh mention also of a proverbial expression generally circulated.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Herein,” He saith, “is that saying true, One soweth and another reapeth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These words the many used whenever one party had supplied toil and another had reaped the fruits; and He saith, “that the proverb is in this instance especially true, for the Prophets labored, and ye reap the fruits of their labors.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not “the rewards,” (for neither did their great labor go unrewarded,) but “the fruits.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This also Daniel did, for he too makes mention of a proverb, “Wickedness proceedeth from the wicked”; and David in his lamenting makes mention of a similar proverb.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore He said beforehand, “that both he that soweth and he that reapeth may rejoice together.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since He was about to declare, that “one hath sowed and another reapeth,” lest any one should deem that the Prophets were deprived of their reward, He asserteth something strange and paradoxical, such as never chanceth in sensual things, but is peculiar to spiritual only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For in things of sense, if it chance that one sow and another reap, they do not “rejoice together,” but those who sowed are sad, as having labored for others, and those who reap alone rejoice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But here it is not so, but those who reap not what they sowed rejoice alike with those who reap; whence it is clear that they too share the reward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I sent you to reap that whereon ye bestowed no labors; other men labored, and ye are entered into their labors.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By this He the more encourageth them; for when it seemed a very hard matter to go through all the world and preach the Gospel, He showeth them that it is even most easy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The very difficult work was that other, which required great labor, the putting in the seed, and introducing the uninitiated soul to the knowledge of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But wherefore uttereth He these sayings?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is that when He sendeth them to preach they may not be confounded, as though sent on a difficult task.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For that of the Prophets,” He saith, “was the more difficult, and the fact witnesseth to My word, that ye are come to what is easy; because as in harvest time the fruits are collected with ease, and in one moment the floor is filled with sheaves, which await not the revolutions of the seasons, and winter, and spring, and rain, so it is now.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The facts proclaim it aloud.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: While He was in the midst of saying these things, the Samaritans came forth, and the fruit was at once gathered together.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account He said, “Lift up your eyes, and look on the fields, that they are white.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus He spake, and the fact was clear, and the words seen (true) by the event.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For saith St. John,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Many of the Samaritans of that city believed on Him for the saying of the woman which testified, He told me all that ever I did.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They perceived that the woman would not from favor have admired One who had rebuked her sins, nor to gratify another have paraded her own course of life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then also imitate this woman, and in the case of our own sins not be ashamed of men, but fear, as is meet, God who now beholdeth what is done, and who hereafter punisheth those who do not now repent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At present we do the opposite of this, for we fear not Him who shall judge us, but shudder at those who do not in anything hurt us, and tremble at the shame which comes from them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore in the very thing which we fear, in this do we incur punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he who now regards only the reproach of men, but when God seeth is not ashamed to do anything unseemly, and who will not repent and be converted, in that day will be made an example, not only before one or two, but in the sight of the whole world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that a vast assembly is seated there to behold righteous actions as well as those which are not such, let the parable of the sheep and the goats teach thee, as also the blessed Paul when He saith, “For we must all appear before the judgment-seat of Christ, that every one may receive the things done in his body, according to that he hath done, whether it be good or bad” ( 2 Cor. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 10 ), and again, “Who will bring to light the hidden things of darkness.” ( 1 Cor. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hast thou done or imagined any evil thing, and dost thou hide it from man? yet from God thou hidest it not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But for this thou careth nothing; the eyes of men, these are thy fear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Think then that thou wilt not be able to escape the sight even of men in that day ; for all things as in a picture shall then be set before our very eyes, so that each shall be self-condemned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is clear even from the instance of Dives, for the poor man whom he had neglected, Lazarus I mean, he saw standing before his eyes, and the finger which he had often loathed, he intreats may become a comfort to him then.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I exhort you therefore, that although no one see what we do, yet that each of us enter into his own conscience, and set reason for his judge, and bring forward his transgressions, and if he desire them not to be exposed to public view then in that fearful day, let him now heal his wounds, let him apply to them the medicines of repentance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it is in the power, yea, it is in the power of one full of ten thousand wounds to go hence whole.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For “if ye forgive,” He saith, “your sins are forgiven unto you.” ( Matt. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 14 , not verbally quoted.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as sins buried in Baptism appear no more, so these also shall disappear, if we be willing to repent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And repentance is the not doing the same again; for he that again puts his hand to the same, is like the dog that returneth to his own vomit, and like him in the proverb who cards wool into the fire, and draws water into a cask full of holes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It behooves therefore to depart both in action and in thought from what we have dared to do, and having departed, to apply to the wounds the remedies which are the contraries of our sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For instance: hast thou been grasping and covetous?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Abstain from rapine, and apply almsgiving to the wound.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hast thou been a fornicator?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Abstain from fornication, and apply chastity to the wound.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hast thou spoken ill of thy brother, and injured him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Cease finding fault, and apply kindness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us thus act with respect to each point in which we have offended, and let us not carelessly pass by our sins, for there awaiteth us hereafter, there awaiteth us a season of account.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore also Paul said, “The Lord is at hand: be careful for nothing.” ( Philip. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But we perhaps must add the contrary of this, “The Lord is at hand, be careful.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they might well hear, “Be careful for nothing,” living as they did in affliction, and labors, and trials; but they who live by rapine, or in luxury, and who shall give a grievous reckoning, would in reason hear not this, but that other, “The Lord is at hand, be careful.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since no long time now remains until the consummation, but the world is hastening to its end; this the wars declare, this the afflictions, this the earthquakes, this the love which hath waxed cold.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as the body when in its last gasp and near to death, draws to itself ten thousand sufferings; and as when a house is about to fall, many portions are wont to fall beforehand from the roof and walls; so is the end of the world nigh and at the very doors, and therefore ten thousand woes are everywhere scattered abroad.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If the Lord was then “at hand,” much more is He now “at hand.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If three hundred years ago, when those words were used, Paul called that season “the fullness of time,” much more would he have called the present so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But perhaps for this very reason some disbelieve, yet they ought on this account to believe the more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For whence knowest thou, O man, that the end is not “at hand,” and the words shortly to be accomplished?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as we speak of the end of the year not as being the last day, but also the last month, though it has thirty days; so if of so many years I call even four hundred years “the end,” I shall not be wrong; and so at that time Paul spoke of the end by anticipation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then set ourselves in order, let us delight in the fear of God; for if we live here without fear of Him, His coming will surprise us suddenly, when we are neither careful, nor looking for Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As Christ declared when He said, “For as in the days of Noah, and as in the days of Lot, so shall it be at the end of this world.” ( Matt. xxiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 37 , not verbally quoted.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This also Paul declared when he said, “For when they shall say, Peace and safety, then sudden destruction cometh upon them, as travail upon a woman with child.” ( 1 Thess. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What means, “as travail upon a woman with child”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Often have pregnant women when sporting, or at their meals, or in the bath or market-place, and foreseeing nothing of what was coming, been seized in a moment by their pains.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now since our case is like theirs, let us ever be prepared, for we shall not always hear these things, we shall not always have power to do them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “In the grave” saith David, “who shall give Thee thanks?” ( Ps. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then repent here, that so we may find God merciful unto us in the day that is to come, and be enabled to enjoy abundant forgiveness; which may we all obtain, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory and dominion now and ever, and world without end.
